role: Author and religion scholar; Professor, UC Riverside
bio:
Reza Aslan is an Iranian-American author, religion scholar, and public intellectual. He was born in Tehran in 1972 and emigrated with his family to the United States after the [1979 Iranian Revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979). He holds a doctorate in the sociology of religion from the University of California, Santa Barbara, and is a professor of creative writing at the University of California, Riverside.

His bestselling books include *No god but God: The Origins, Evolution, and Future of Islam* (2005), which became one of the most widely read popular accounts of Islamic history and theology; and *Zealot: The Life and Times of Jesus of Nazareth* (2013), which was a major commercial success. He is also the author of *Beyond Fundamentalism: Confronting Religious Extremism in the Age of Globalization* (2010) and *God: A Human History* (2017).

Aslan is primarily a religion scholar and public communicator rather than a specialist on contemporary Iranian politics. His commentary on Iran is typically framed from a moderate-to-dovish perspective, opposing military intervention and sanctions escalation and contextualizing IRI behavior within broader patterns of religious nationalism. He produced the CNN docuseries *Believer* (2017) exploring religious traditions around the world.

He was a board member of the National Iranian American Council ([NIAC](/orgs/niac)) for several years, which is relevant context for understanding his Iran policy positioning.
